title: artemisia tilesii subsp. typica
intro: Artemisia tilesii subsp. typica, also known as the western wormwood, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to repel insects and treat digestive issues. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for reducing pain, fever, and skin irritations, as well as promoting wound healing and immune system function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the western wormwood can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a variety of health issues, including colds, flu, and digestive problems.
